What exactly is Apartment IQ?
How do I use it to help me save time and make data informed decisions.
Where can I access answers to common questions and then who do I contact if I need help?

Now, before we get started, I did have really great conversations with Aissa Michael Zach uh in regards to your current processes and I am aware that your teams uh right now have more of like a manual process for collecting data.
And I know in terms of some concerns private data sharing, just the sheer time it to collect that information uh is a concern.
So these questions are here simply just as a prompt uh for us to kind of get our brain thinking because apartment IQ, the ultimate goal is to save you as well as your on site teams.
A ton of time uh from having to call around stop by manually collect information.
Uh Apartment IQ is going to streamline that process for you.
So part IQ is an automated market survey tool that's updated every 24 hours leveraging public data.
The great thing about our tool is that it's not a partnership ecosystem where I have to provide data and then another person provides data and we kind of data swap.
Uh This tool is leveraging 100% public information updated every 24 hours.
So it's really easy to stay on top of what's happening in your market.
We also offer the largest competitive database on the market today.
We're tracking currently around 25 million units and 85,000 properties in the United States.
So our coverage is very strong and if there's any properties that are missing, perhaps they are a brand new lease up or something that we just haven't tracked before.
Uh We're happy to get that built out for you in our system.
Now, apartment IQ offers a couple different user roles and I wanted to turn it back over to Michael and Zach just to kind of chat with you.
Uh in regards to the permissions that your team is going to have here.
Yeah, I'm happy to address that.
Thanks.
Um So for the most part, uh the majority of uh the users on this call are gonna have either the the editor or the manager role.
And so you can see uh the persona listed at the bottom there, help you have gain a better understanding of what your access is gonna be.
Um We have limited the admin access to just a select few users.
Um Main main point being who's able to add and remove users, right?
And so if you're on an editor role, you can still edit cops, which is update the, the properties you're responsible for.
And the same applies to the manager and admin uh roles here.

So, uh if you have any questions or feel you've been assigned a role that's not appropriate for your position, please just reach out we can have a conversation and make any necessary changes at that point.
Awesome.
Thank you so much.

Again?

So Apartment IQ is broken down into several reports.

The first report that we're looking here is the market survey and this is gonna give access to information regarding occupancy application, metrics, concessions, fees, deposits, amenities, all those great features that we know your on site teams need to provide uh to key stakeholders on a weekly basis.
Now, in addition to that, we also have a floor plan report.
This is a brand new report we released about a month ago.
This report is going to showcase information regarding individualized floor plan metrics, tracking that data over time.
So we actually have access to up to about three years of data.
Uh going back to about 2020 here.
So we're approaching very quickly four years of data uh regarding your overall market.
Our teams really love this report because you're able to compare apples to apples, really dig into how your floor plans directly compare to cops.

Now, the units report is going to be a great place for folks that are on site to go when they need to understand what units have leased or become available in the last 30 days for uh uh on site teams purposes.
This report is really popular for property managers, leasing specialists because they can very easily see what's available, what the amenities are and provide value add.
Um tips to potential uh renters as well.

Now, I also think your team is gonna be really excited to see this historicals model because this is going to provide you insight into uh historical data for pricing exposure metrics availability and leasing activity in the last three years.
Uh You'll be able to toggle between property level metrics as well as individualized floor plans as well.
All right.

But every individual user can actually customize the metrics that they're seeing here.
So when they come in and log into their system, if things like total gross rent, that effective rent are important to them, they can enable that to be seen upon login.
I commonly recommend that folks include things like exposure, metrics, total availability and vacancy rates, uh as well as real listed activity and then concessions
once you have all of the metrics uh situated here, a couple of things I want to point out you can hover over metrics to have it explained to you how that's being calculated.
Uh I highly encourage you to do that just to get uh your footing in terms of how these metrics are calculated.
But if you have any further questions, I would strongly encourage you to check out our help center.

I have some frequently asked questions, a glossary there for you and then any kind of product updates that are occurring over time will be published as well within this help center.
So a couple that I commonly get questions about are things like applications.
